extra.message: User-controlled 'post_id' is concatenated directly into a SQL query string passed to conn.execute(). Although a weak regex is used, concatenating user input into SQL is a classic SQL injection pattern and the regex with re.M can be bypassed via multi-line input.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: Hardcoded Flask SECRET_KEY 'qwerty12345' in source code. An attacker can forge session cookies and flash messages.
Hardcoded Flask SECRET_KEY '55555' in source code. An attacker who knows this value can forge session cookies.

urllib.request.urlopen() is called on a user-supplied URL (request.args.get('url')). The startswith('https://insecurewebapp.com') prefix check can be bypassed (e.g., 'https://insecurewebapp.com.attacker.com' or user-info like 'https://insecurewebapp.com@attacker.com'), allowing Server-Side Request Forgery.

User-controlled self.path is appended to os.getcwd() and then open()ed and returned to the client. The regex check r'/\.\.\/\.\.\/' is trivially bypassed (e.g. by using a single ../ or URL-encoded sequences), enabling arbitrary local file read (path traversal / LFI).

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: The server reads the 'username' cookie from the client and passes it to pickle-based deserialization (Username.pickle() / pickle.loads on cookie value). Deserializing attacker-controlled pickle data leads to arbitrary code execution.
The 'username' value derived from request data is interpolated into the HTML response without escaping, enabling reflected/stored XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The decrypted cookie-derived 'username' value is written directly into the HTML response without HTML-escaping, enabling Cross-Site Scripting if an attacker can control the decrypted value.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: AES-CBC is used for session cookies without any authentication (MAC). This allows padding-oracle attacks and bit-flipping against the session cookie.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: Signature is computed as SHA-256(secret || payment_information). SHA-256 is vulnerable to length-extension attacks when used in this Secret-Prefix MAC construction; an HMAC should be used instead. An attacker can append data to payment_information and produce a valid signature.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: Signature computed as SHA-256(secret || payment_information) is vulnerable to length-extension attacks. Use HMAC-SHA256 instead.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: User-controlled payment params are concatenated into a redirect target URL. While the host is fixed, parameters including user-controlled values are reflected into the redirect URL without validation.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: SSLContext.check_hostname is set to False, disabling TLS hostname verification. This enables man-in-the-middle attacks.
HTTP server binds to 0.0.0.0, exposing the service on all network interfaces.

cwe: CWE-1327: Binding to an Unrestricted IP Address
